sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

In the Qwen image preprocessing, there is a permute operation with more than 8 dimensions that is not supported on the NPU. Currently, SGLang directly processes this operation on the CPU, whose efficiency is much lower than that on the NPU. This PR will patch the operations unsupported b

sglang.title: [Utils] Add `NetworkAddress` abstraction for IPv6-safe address handling

sglang.bodyExcerpt:

In multi-tokenizer-worker mode, specifically under sparse traffic scenarios, health-check requests may be ignored or not properly routed back to the tokenizer.

sglang.title: fix: gracefully abort last request in retract_decode on OOM
sglang.author: alphabetc1
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When a single decode request exhausts the KV cache pool (e.g. with paged EAGLE speculative decoding), retract_decode has no other requests to retract and raises ValueError due to OOM.
BTW, the real cause of the OOM is #20049, but I think it’s still necessary to add a fallback safeguard.
M

vllm.author: sfeng33
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
We currently have no good way to measure whether tool-calling accuracy improves or regresses across vLLM changes. This script provides a reproducible CI benchmark using BFCL (Berkeley Function Call Leaderboard) so that tool-call parser bug fixes and improvements can be validated with concret

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix negative max_tokens when input prompt is too long
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

get_max_tokens will return negative max_tokens when max_model_len too small, which makes error message a bit confusing

openai.BadRequestError: Error code: 400 - {'error': {'message': 'max_tokens must be at least 1, got -7584. (parameter=max_tokens, value=-7584)', 'type': 'BadRequestError',

vllm.title: [Misc] Clean up renderers
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

Resolve model-based tokenizer_mode during config construction instead of when initializing the tokenizer, to ensure that both tokenizer and renderer are reading the correct value in the config.
Generalize MM processing tests to avoid model-specific code
Fix missed copyright attribution docs

vllm.title: Fix `ExaoneMoeMTP` test that never ran in Transformers v4
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This model asserts that prefix caching is disabled but this was not the case when the test tried to instantiate it. This PR fixes that.

vllm.title: fix: align lfm2 thumbnail token counting with HF
vllm.author: tianshu-Michael-yu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Align LFM2-VL thumbnail token counting with Hugging Face behavior.
Change

update thumbnail token counting logic in vllm/model_executor/models/lfm2_vl.py

vllm.title: [KVConnector] Support worker -> scheduler metadata
vllm.author: orozery
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR introduces a new build_worker_connector_meta KV connector API,
allowing workers to send back arbitrary metadata back to the scheduler-side connector.
This aligns with the already existing API build_connector_metadata which allows for the same on the opposite direction (scheduler -> worker).

vllm.title: [torch.compile] Add support for non-contiguous fused RMSNorm + group quant
vllm.author: ProExpertProg
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Background
Fused rms_norm + group fp8 quant kernel only supports contiguous inputs. This is an issue in the Deepseek case, because the norm input is a slice of the qkv_lora tensor:
vllm/model_executor/layers/mla.py:134-139:
q_c, kv_lora = qkv_lora.split(
    [self.q_lora_rank, self.kv_lora_rank + se

vllm.title: Make Gemma and Gemma 2 accept `inputs_embeds` like Gemma 3
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Gemma3Model.forward accepts pre-scaled inputs_embeds which are scaled by Gemma3Model.embed_input_ids.
Before this PR GemmaModel and Gemma2Model did the scaling inside forward.
After this PR the scaling for the earlier Gemma variants happens in embed_input_ids. This is consistent with:

Gemma3
Gemma

vllm.title: [ROCm] add tuned moe_wna16_triton kernel configs for CDNA4
vllm.author: amd-asalykov
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Improve Kimi K2.5 prefill and decode perf. on CDNA4 by including tuned kernel configs for fused_moe_kernel_gptq_awq
Test Plan

Purpose
Remove dead code in kv connector

vllm.title: [BugFix]: add bagel to MM_PREFIX_LM_MODELS
vllm.author: princepride
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add bagel to the MM_PREFIX_LM_MODELS list to support it as a prefix-capable multimodal language model.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][ROCm] Strip block_size before attention backend validation
vllm.author: jennyyyyzhen
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The ROCm attention backend refactor (#35246) introduced validate_configuration calls that reject irregular block_size because it is not in the BlockSize type (1, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256). The CUDA platform avoids this by stripping block_size before validation. Apply the same fix to the ROCm

vllm.title: [BugFix][kv_offload] Fix offloading decodes with async scheduling
vllm.author: orozery
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR fixes OffloadingConnector to offload also decoded tokens, which were previously skipped as their block hash was not available. This issue only occurs when async scheduling is enabled.

vllm.title: [LMCache] Pass TP size in lookup for MLA multi-reader locking
vllm.author: maobaolong
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
When MLA is enabled, the world_size passed to LMCache is divided by tp_size (since all TP ranks share the same KV cache in MLA models). However, the read lock count during lookup still needs the original TP size to acquire the correct number of locks for all workers that will independently r

vllm.title: [Model Runner V2] Add probabilistic rejection sampling for spec decoding
vllm.author: TheEpicDolphin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Use probabilistic rejection sampling for MRV2 spec decoding to increase draft token acceptance rate.
Currently, strict rejection sampling is being used, where draft tokens are accepted only if they exactly match the target model's sampled tokens. This ensures that the target model's distribu

vllm.title: [Model Runner V2] Add WhisperModelState [6/N]
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Should be merged after #35774

vllm.title: [Kernel] [Helion] [14/N] Set autotune_ignore_errors=True during autotuning
vllm.author: gmagogsfm
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This allows the autotuner to get over recoverable compilation/runtime errors caused by exploring bad configs or Triton bugs, instead of aborting the entire autotune search.

vllm.title: [Kernel] [Helion] [12/N] Use FakeTensorMode to avoid GPU allocation during config key computation
vllm.author: gmagogsfm
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Optimize autotune script loading time by avoiding allocating real tensors when computing config keys. This would allow us to spend very little time for kernels that have 0 configs that need autotuning, which should be true for majority of kernels in the future.

vllm.title: [Kernel] [Helion] [15/N] Split config files into per-platform files
vllm.author: gmagogsfm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Change helion config storage from a single monolithic JSON file per kernel to a directory of per-platform JSON files, so each file stays manageable as more platforms are added.
Before: configs/silu_mul_fp8.json
After:  configs/silu_mul_fp8/nvidia_h100.json + nvidia_h200.json

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI/Build] Add gfx1152/gfx1153 (Krackan) to HIP supported architectures
vllm.author: mgehre-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Without these entries, the CMake build silently drops gfx1152/gfx1153 from PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH even though they are valid RDNA 3.5 targets, resulting in wheels that lack kernel images for these GPUs.
I have a validated a local vLLM build with this fix on gfx1153.

vllm.author: jikunshang

Purpose
Intel OneAPI no longer needs to be installed separately — it is pulled in automatically as a dependency of torch-xpu. Manual installation is redundant and potentially confusing.
Changes
Requirements section: Removed OneAPI requirements: oneAPI 2025.3 bullet
Build from source: Replaced the "i

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix minimax_m2 tool parser when stream interval > 1
vllm.author: sfeng33
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix MiniMax M2 tool parser dropping arguments when stream_interval > 1.
Background
MiniMax M2 uses XML-based tool call format:
<minimax:tool_call>
<invoke name="get_weather">
<parameter name="city">Seattle</parameter>
<parameter name="days">5</parameter>
</invoke>
</minimax:tool_call>

The o

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix multiple/duplicate stdout prefixes
vllm.author: njhill

Also includes changes to omit _DP0 suffix when dp isn't in use (dp size == 1).
DP rank will already be reflected in worker suffix for dp > 1.
And includes some adjacent code simplification.
Before:

After:

vllm.title: [Kernel][Helion][13/N] Force static_shapes=False in helion register
vllm.author: gmagogsfm
vllm.bodyExcerpt: vLLM requires dynamic shapes for variable batch sizes and sequence lengths. Previously, static_shapes was only set to False as a default and could be overridden to True by user settings. Now it is always forced to False with a warning if the user explicitly requested True.

vllm.title: [Tests] Skip model weight download for render-only test server
vllm.author: sagearc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
RemoteLaunchRenderServer inherits the full model weight download from RemoteVLLMServer.__init__, but the render server only needs the tokenizer — no weights are loaded at runtime.
Changes

Extract _pre_download_model hook from RemoteVLLMServer.__init__
Override it as a no-op in RemoteLaunchR
